generated.clinical_history: Correlate clinically with jugular catheter and pulmonary nodules
generated.findings: Right internal jugular central catheter, distal tip in right atrium; bilateral pulmonary nodules suggesting metastatic disease to lungs; normal heart size and mediastinal contour; no focal airspace consolidation, pleural effusion, or pneumothorax; mild degenerative changes of the spine
generated.impression: No acute cardiopulmonary findings; bilateral pulmonary nodules suggesting pulmonary metastases
generated.recommendation: Contrast-enhanced imaging would be suited to evaluate these findings

ground_truth.findings: No focal consolidation, suspicious pulmonary opacity, pneumothorax or definite pleural effusion. Heart size and pulmonary vascularity within normal limits, visualized osseous structures appear intact.
